Hi everyone I am CEO of my hair and have previously gained extensive experience as CFO of Piggy Bank© (before it broke). I graduated from MIT and use arch btw (not really, I just wanna sound cool).
I am here to make new nakama-tachi and find a new tribe. And have fun. Because who wants to go to a park, duh.

Heyo, I’m anotherFool, I’m curious about learning tech stuff.

yo 🧠 I’m Rudra – officially a BTech student, unofficially a tech squirrel 🐿️. I switch domains faster than Chrome tabs at 3AM: one day it’s game dev, next day it’s React dashboards, then suddenly I'm decoding blockchains like I understand them 😭. DSA? Web3? Unity? Godot? Obsidian brain dumps? yes. all. at once. If it looks shiny, I’m in. Let's build stuff and pretend we know what we're doing 💀🚀
Yo fellows. Call me nathuram i dont wanna get doxxed. Im interested in programming and all kinds of logical stuff/puzzles. Hoping to enjoy and learn and grow with y'all!!

Hey! I’d love to sync up and study together for DSA and Web Dev — sounds like we’re on a similar path. Just to get to know you better and make sure we’re aligned, mind if I ask a few things? Like, how much progress have you made so far in both? Are you currently in college or already passed out — and which college, if you're cool sharing? Also, how old are you? Just so we can match pace better. Lastly, what’s your realistic goal for this year in terms of DSA/Web Dev — like aiming for an internship, placement, or mastering core concepts? Would be awesome to grow together with clarity. Let me know! 😊

messages.content: Hey @everyone, I’m Himanshu, an anime and cinema nut. I’m currently doing my degree in arts (not fine arts). About a year ago, I switched from browsing on my phone to using my laptop, and since then, I’ve been using it all the time for everyday stuff. A month ago, I discovered different operating systems and ricing, and it seemed so cool that I installed Arch Linux with i3 WM. I’ve been using them ever since. I don’t know programming yet, but I’m thinking of giving it a try, so I also installed Neovim. That’s all the computer knowledge I have, lol.

hello, CS student here, trying to get the hell out of loops of CS...
Hey guys, I am a CS graduate trying to find my calling. I am here to get learn and master my craft.

This is my first project in web dev. I made a website for my favourite character from gintama. But now I waant to ask how I can make it more impressive. 

As of now, I am using Next.js and deployed it on vercel. It has some basic videos stuff, mcq games and quote. And the most interesting part is chatbot  via Grok API. 


But I want to learn more about software architecture and while process of deploying, adding features, testing ect. Below is link to my website. 
Btw I'm all for new projects, if someone want to suggest please do. I am at this point where I know some things about everything but not at all master of single thing and that's why I am struggling to land a job. I'm open to all new challenges and projects.

If someone could guide to contribute to open source then to would be great..I am researching online too but some personal advice will go a long way. Thanks you very much for responding to my post
